personal.name: Meysam Najafitavani
personal.title: Senior Frontend Engineer
personal.summary: Senior Frontend Engineer with 8+ years of experience building scalable web applications using React, TypeScript, Next.js, and modern frontend technologies. Experienced in Microfrontend Architecture, Module Federation, AEM, and enterprise application development. Passionate about creating high-performance, user-friendly, and maintainable solutions while collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver business value.

experience.description: Humana is a leading U.S. healthcare and health insurance company serving millions of members through Medicare, Medicaid, and commercial health plans.
responsibilities: Led React microfrontend development using Next.js, Vite, and Module Federation.
Published and consumed remote modules through Module Federation Catalog.
Built reusable Lit Web Components and shared frontend utilities.
Maintained global SCSS and Tailwind CSS architecture.
Integrated AEM-driven module fragments for content-managed experiences.
Supported technical guidance, onboarding, and feature delivery planning.

experience.description: Infosys is a global technology consulting and digital services company that helps enterprises modernize applications, cloud infrastructure, and business processes.
responsibilities: Developed mobile-first UI using React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and SCSS.
Built reusable components with scalable frontend architecture.
Converted Figma designs into production-ready UI components.
Integrated REST APIs and optimized frontend performance.
Applied accessibility and responsive design best practices.

experience.description: The Search Optimization Team brings new tech modernization and advanced technologies into various aspects of CVS codebase. This includes upgrading and improving systems, processes, and infrastructure to increase the efficiency of websites.
responsibilities: Developed React, Redux, TypeScript, Stencil, Tailwind, and SASS applications.
Integrated Contentful CMS for dynamic content management.
Built reusable components across multiple microfrontend repositories.
Improved CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins, Harness, GitHub Actions, and JFrog.
Optimized performance, SEO, analytics tracking, and GTM implementation.
Completed unit and E2E testing using Jest and React Testing Library.

experience.description: Developed and enhanced educational data visualization applications that helped educators track student performance and make data-driven decisions.
responsibilities: Developed scalable SPAs using React, Redux, TypeScript, and Material UI.
Built dashboards and reusable UI components with React Hooks and Highcharts.
Integrated REST APIs and client-side routing.
Improved performance, accessibility, SEO, and analytics tracking.

experience.description: Developed and maintained responsive React-based websites and UI components for jewelry industry clients, focusing on product integration, performance, and user experience.
responsibilities: Built mobile-first responsive interfaces using React, JavaScript, HTML, and CSS.
Implemented client-side validation with React, JavaScript, GraphQL, and jQuery.
Created cross-browser compatible layouts using CSS3 and Bootstrap.
Worked with clients to gather requirements and support website delivery.

education.description: HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, React, Node.js, MongoDB, Express, MySQL
Web development, server-side programming, and website administration

projects.title: Compliance Portal
projects.description: Full-stack compliance portal with authentication, document management, and role-based access control.

projects.title: Fitness App
projects.description: Responsive fitness application with training schedules and product catalog.

interests.paragraph1: Outside of software development, I enjoy exploring the outdoors through camping, hiking, fishing, and road trips across California and the western United States.
interests.paragraph2: I also enjoy working on personal technology projects, learning new web technologies, and building applications with modern frontend frameworks.
